majors.name: Electrical Engineering
majors.rawMinGpa: 2.75 minimum cumulative GPA for transfer consideration (3.0 often required for dual admissions/competitive programs)
majors.rawRequired: Pre-calculus or Calculus (equivalent to MATH 121), Chemistry with Lab (CHEM 101), Physics with Lab (PHYS 141). At least two must be completed at the time of application (one of which must be pre-calculus or calculus).

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.rawMinGpa: 2.00 minimum cumulative GPA for transfer admission
requiredCourses: COM S 227 - Introduction to Object-oriented Programming
COM S 228 - Introduction to Data Structures
recommendedCourses: ENGL 2500 - Written, Oral, Visual, and Electronic Composition
majors.rawRequired:
COM S 227 - Introduction to Object-oriented Programming (Grade C- or higher)
COM S 228 - Introduction to Data Structures (Grade C- or higher)
MATH 165 - Calculus I (Grade C- or higher)
majors.rawRecommended:
MATH 166 - Calculus II
ENGL 1500 - Critical Thinking and Communication
ENGL 2500 - Written, Oral, Visual, and Electronic Composition
COM S 230 - Discrete Computational Structures

english.raw: TOEFL iBT: 71 (subscores 17+ on speaking/writing) or 4.0 overall band (subscores 3.5+) on/after Jan 21, 2026 | IELTS: 6.0 (subscores 5.5+) | Duolingo DET: 105

majors.englishExemption: Waiver granted if native English speaker, graduated from U.S. high school, or completed equivalent of English 1500 or 2500 with a B or higher at a U.S. college.
majors.note: Verified: Prerequisite requirements confirmed against Iowa State Department of Computer Science guidelines and ISU catalog.

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.rawMinGpa: 2.25 minimum cumulative GPA for transfer consideration (2.00 for applicants with an associate's degree)
requiredCourses: CHEM 1670 - General Chemistry for Engineering (or CHEM 1770)
ENGR 1600 - Engineering Problems with Computer Applications Laboratory
recommendedCourses: PHYS 2310 - Introduction to Classical Physics I
PHYS 2310L - Introduction to Classical Physics I Laboratory
majors.rawRequired: Basic Program courses must be completed with C or better: MATH 1650 - Calculus I, MATH 1660 - Calculus II, ENGL 1500 - Critical Thinking and Communication, CHEM 1670 - General Chemistry (or CHEM 1770), ENGR 1010 - Engineering Orientation, ENGR 1600 - Engineering Problems, LIB 1600 - Information Literacy.
majors.rawRecommended:
PHYS 2310 - Introduction to Classical Physics I
PHYS 2310L - Introduction to Classical Physics I Laboratory
